---
title: "Learning to Reason in 13 Parameters"
description: "TinyLoRA: an 8B Qwen2.5 reaches 91% on GSM8K with only 13 trained bf16 parameters — 26 bytes of learned weights."
author: "synesis"
date: "2026-03-31"
categories: [AI, LLMs, reasoning, generative AI, fine-tuning, links]
image: figure1.png
---

![Figure 1 from the TinyLoRA paper. ([Source](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.04118))](figure1.png){fig-align="center"}

TinyLoRA pushes low-rank adaptation down to almost nothing [[1]](#ref-1).

* They report that an 8B-parameter Qwen2.5 model reaches 91% on GSM8K with only 13 trained bf16 parameters, which they note is just 26 bytes of learned weights.
* For RL-based post-training, the effective update needed to unlock better reasoning may live in a very low-dimensional subspace.
* This works well with RL, but not nearly as well with SFT, where they say SFT needs 100–1000x larger updates to match the same gains.

---

## References

<a id="ref-1"></a>[1] "Learning to Reason in 13 Parameters." *arXiv*. <https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.04118>

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_learning-to-reason-in-13-parameters-activity-7444959168408559616-psHg).*
